name: LEAP API Reference
description: # Getting Started To start working on your LEAP Marketplace application, sign up for a LEAP Developer Console account. You will be prompted to enter some details about your organisation and complete tasks such as verifying your email. Our marketplace team will reach out to you shortly after receiving your application to assist you in moving forward to the pre-approval stage. # Overview Using the LEAP API, you can build applications that integrate directly with other LEAP products. This guide explains how to get started with the TEST API, describes each of the available resources, and provides example code for same. Each resource type is accessible through a variety of HTTP (GET | POST | PUT | DELETE) operators. Within each resource type, endpoints fall into two categories: **{base url}/{resource type}:** * Retrieves the complete data set for each resource of a specified type. This data can be filtered with the addition of query parameters. **{base url}/{resource type}/{resourceid}:** * Retrieves the data set for a single specific resource. # Caching The LEAP API employs caching for certain endpoints to optimize data retrieval and processing efficiency. By allowing frequently requested information to be temporarily stored and quickly accessed, caching reduces the need for redundant calculations and network requests, ultimately enhancing overall performance and responsiveness of the platform. By default, caching is disabled for an endpoint unless specified otherwise. # API Changelog All changes to the API services are loged and tracked via in internal service. This service is attached to a notification system, which will soon be available for registration. # API Domains The LEAP API supports multiple regions and environments. Please ensure that you are using the correct domain for each request. | Region | Environment | Domain | | :------------:|:-------------:|:--------------------------| | AU | Sandbox/Live | au-api.leap.services | | | LiveB | au-liveb-api.leap.services | | CA | Sandbox/Live | ca-api.leap.services | | UK | Sandbox/Live | uk-api.leap.services | | US | Sandbox/Live | us-api.leap.services | # API Technical Specs API technical specs are available on the following swagger pages: | Region | Environment | Domain | | :------------:|:-------------:|:--------------------------| | AU | Sandbox/Live | https://api-swagger-au-advanced.leap.services/#/ | | | LiveB | https://api-swagger-au-liveb-advanced.leap.services/#/ | | CA | Sandbox/Live | https://api-swagger-ca-advanced.leap.services/#/ | | UK | Sandbox/Live | https://api-swagger-uk-advanced.leap.services/#/ | | US | Sandbox/Live | https://api-swagger-us-advanced.leap.services/#/ | # API Throttling To prevent malicious usage of our API, we limit the number of requests (per second), that can be made. You can avoid reaching this limit by implementing proper caching strategies within your client applcation. Should the limit be reached, the API will generate and respond to any request with an HTTP error code of 429 (too many requests). This limit is short lived and should be resolved by simply retrying within a second.
